steps.title: Calculate sample proportion and scale to population
steps.content:
The sample data shows 24 out of 96 customers purchased sandwiches. This gives a sample proportion of:
$$\frac{24}{96} = \frac{1}{4} = 0.25$$
To estimate how many of the total 1,800 customers purchased sandwiches, apply this proportion to the full population:
$$1,800 \times \frac{1}{4} = 450 \text{ customers}$$

steps.title: Identify appropriate language and select the choice
steps.content:
When inferring population characteristics from a sample, the correct statistical language is best estimate, not exactly must have. Sample-based conclusions always carry uncertainty because we observed only a portion of the population.

This eliminates those choices because both incorrectly claim the count must be exact. Between the remaining choices:
  • Choice C — The best estimate for the number of customers who purchased sandwiches last Friday is 75. Estimates 75 (incorrect count)
  • Choice D — The best estimate for the number of customers who purchased sandwiches last Friday is 450. Estimates 450 (matches our calculation) with appropriate statistical language
That confirms D is correct.
